# founder-skills

Structured, source-grounded skill files distilled from founder knowledge sources (starting
with YC's CS183B "How to Start a Startup," Stanford 2014). Each line below is one skill file
and when to load it. Read `NORTH_STAR.md` for the standard every file is held to.

/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/market-strategy/competition-monopoly-thiel.md — load when a founder is deciding whether to enter a market, evaluating defensibility, choosing between ideas, prioritizing a roadmap, or asking why a competitor couldn't just copy them.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/hiring/team-and-cofounders-altman.md — load when a founder is choosing a cofounder, evaluating an early hire, setting equity or vesting, or deciding whether to fire someone who isn't working out.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/operations/execution-altman.md — load when a founder can't decide what to focus on, growth has stalled, a competitor's press is demoralizing the team, or speed and quality feel like a tradeoff.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/ideas/idea-and-product-altman.md — load when a founder is choosing between ideas, doubting whether an idea is any good, questioning market timing, or has users who like but don't love the product.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/ideas/why-start-a-startup-moskovitz.md — load when someone is deciding whether to start a company at all, drawn in for flexibility/status/money, or unsure their motivation will survive a multi-year grind.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/fundraising/what-investors-want-andreessen-conway.md — load when a founder is preparing to pitch, deciding how much to raise, comparing investors or term sheets, or worried a lack of traction disqualifies them from raising.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/product/building-for-love-hale.md — load when a founder is deciding what to build next from feature requests, users are lukewarm rather than attached, marketing spend feels like a leak, or support volume is piling up.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/user-research/user-interviews-shear.md — load when a founder doesn't know who to interview, built a requested feature nobody used, is choosing between researching users vs. competitors' users vs. non-users, or can't get the team to believe research findings.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/hiring/culture-chesky-lin.md — load when a founder is writing core values for the first time, weighing a candidate's culture fit against their skills, facing a decision where pragmatism and values conflict, or deciding if culture work is worth the time cost right now.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/growth/unscalable-things-williams-tang.md — load when a founder is testing an idea before building real infrastructure, stuck on getting the first users, wondering when to stop doing manual/unscalable work, or facing a speed-vs-clean-code tradeoff.
/skills/yc-how-to-start-a-startup/growth/getting-press-kan.md — load when a founder wants press coverage without a clear reason, is deciding whether to hire a PR firm, doesn't know how to reach a reporter, or is treating coverage as a proxy for success.
